description_long: This fence consists of four vertical posts made of a brown material, likely wood, supporting two wide, horizontal rails that are painted a muted green. The rails are offset in a staggered arrangement, creating a visually interesting, slightly irregular pattern. The posts are rectangular in cross-section and extend slightly above the top rail, with flat tops and no decorative caps. The construction appears sturdy and functional, suitable for demarcating boundaries in urban parks, gardens, or along sidewalks. There are no visible fasteners or embellishments, giving the fence a minimalist, utilitarian appearance. The overall finish is matte, with a hard, slightly textured surface typical of painted or treated wood.
description: A minimalist fence with four brown vertical posts and two staggered green horizontal rails, featuring a flat top and no decorative elements. The design is functional and sturdy, suitable for urban or park settings.
description_view_0: Front view showing all four brown posts and the two staggered green rails, clearly displaying the offset pattern.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the fence and the depth of the rails and posts.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the alignment of the posts and rails, with the staggered pattern less pronounced.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, highlighting the narrowness and construction of the fence.
